# Source Card: Anemia in the pediatric patient

## Summary
Gallagher's 2022 Blood (ASH) Review Series article establishes the morphology-first, reticulocyte-response diagnostic framework for pediatric anemia that the anemia KB record BLOOD2022_PED_ANEMIA backs: classify by MCV (microcytic/normocytic/macrocytic), then refine by reticulocyte count, with peripheral smear and multi-lineage-cytopenia findings guiding escalation. It is authoritative for the diagnostic APPROACH and differential structure; it is not a source of age-specific numeric hemoglobin or MCV diagnostic cutoffs (only the normocytic MCV 80-100 fL band is stated verbatim).

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Morphology-first + reticulocyte-response classification framework — locator: Section "Classification of anemia" / Figure 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:2b4c9ecb9a7d5f9759c10ab238d359d7c083cdf9757c56ad401820201a7de28b]"
- (ev_002) Normocytic MCV band 80-100 fL (only explicit numeric range) — locator: Section "Normocytic anemia" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:18db9fb969e9f2f8ca9e9857e7bc8ff45cef53beef70295db69193ca89e40610]"
- (ev_003) Reticulocyte direction distinguishes production response — locator: Section "Laboratory investigation" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0613df14630df6e19383f76c8e3acadd47727c7a5af10bf62fd7727350003fd4]"
- (ev_004) Peripheral smear morphology → hemolytic mechanism — locator: Section "Laboratory investigation" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:6f534c68c0e8d403b6bbb622c98089b9ada9888ce5d27e0745ae27abdf7719c9]"
- (ev_005) Pancytopenia / marrow-replacement smear pattern — locator: Section "BM replacement syndromes" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f48c2451293e5cf1a25b7939c8068c4c42179bad5abf68c65a470d0a34371478]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: infants and children with anemia (individual-patient diagnostic workup; the review also covers population-level nutritional anemia).
- Assay/method dependence: MCV, reticulocyte count, and CBC indices are automated-analyzer-reported; smear findings are microscopy-based. The single numeric anchor (MCV 80-100 fL, ev_002) is analyzer-dependent, and the review's own Figure 1 / Table 1 show pediatric MCV reference limits are age-dependent — so 80-100 fL is a general band, not an age-specific cutoff.
- Lifecycle: guideline version = Blood 2022 Review Series (Blood 2022;140(6):571-593); effective 2022-08; no retirement/supersession noted.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes. The multi-lineage cytopenia / marrow-replacement pattern (ev_005) and the smear morphology cues (ev_004) support the KB's escalation rules (ALERT-004 multilineage cytopenia, ALERT-005 blasts) that broaden concern beyond isolated nutritional anemia and prompt expert hematology/oncology review. The source frames these as diagnostic signals; the KB's specific alert/referral wording is an implementation proposal layered on top, not stated verbatim in the source.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: The KB uses microcytic (MCV < ~80 fL) and macrocytic (MCV > ~100 fL) cutoffs, but this source states ONLY the normocytic band (MCV 80-100 fL) verbatim; the explicit microcytic/macrocytic numeric MCV cutoffs are NOT locatable in Gallagher 2022 and must not be asserted from this source (threshold "MCV < 80 fL" / "MCV > 100 fL" not locatable in source BLOOD2022_PED_ANEMIA).
- GAP: WHO age-specific hemoglobin diagnostic cutoffs are NOT in this source (Table 1 gives mean ± SD infant values, not diagnostic thresholds); the KB backs those with WHO2024_HB, not this record.
- GAP: The Hb < 7 g/dL severe-anemia threshold (KB rule ALERT-003) is attributed in the KB to AAP2026_IDA, not to this source; not locatable verbatim in Gallagher 2022 as a threshold.
- Conflict (metadata): PMC9373018 metadata DOI (10.1182/blood.2020006479) differs from the KB record's cited DOI (10.1182/blood.2021013018). Both point to the same PMC full text titled "Anemia in the pediatric patient"; the discrepancy is preserved here, not silently reconciled. Card uses the KB record's DOI per instruction.
- Scope: Narrative review — provides a diagnostic framework and differential structure, not machine-executable rules. This card is a PROPOSAL for the CDS converter, not a diagnosis/treatment/transfusion directive.
